title: Jakarta EE
source: jakarta.ee specifications + Jakarta EE Tutorial (eclipse-ee4j) + Baeldung; Spring contrast throughout

items.label: Jakarta EE (ex Java EE), the namespace change, specs umbrella, profiles (Core/Web/Platform), Jakarta EE 11
Application servers (WildFly/Payara/Open Liberty/GlassFish) vs Spring, runtimes and portability
@Inject, bean discovery, qualifiers, @Named
Scopes (@RequestScoped/@SessionScoped/@ApplicationScoped/@Dependent/@ConversationScoped), contexts
@Produces + disposers, @Alternative, CDI events (@Observes, async events)
Interceptors and decorators, lifecycle callbacks (@PostConstruct/@PreDestroy)
Servlets, request/response, servlet lifecycle, the servlet container
Filters, listeners, sessions, servlet vs Spring MVC DispatcherServlet
@Path, HTTP method annotations, path/query/header params, @Produces/@Consumes, JSON-B
Application/Resource configuration, implementations (Jersey/RESTEasy), Spring MVC contrast
Providers (MessageBodyReader/Writer), ExceptionMapper, filters and interceptors
JAX-RS Client API, async and reactive, SSE
Container-managed EntityManager, @PersistenceContext, persistence.xml, persistence units
Contrast with Spring Data JPA (links the Hibernate/JPA track for mapping depth)
Jakarta Transactions (JTA), @Transactional (jakarta), UserTransaction, container-managed vs bean-managed
Enterprise Beans: stateless/stateful/singleton session beans, contrast Spring beans + @Transactional
Constraint annotations, @Valid, cascading, validation groups
Custom constraints (ConstraintValidator), method validation, integration with JAX-RS/CDI
Component model, Facelets, managed beans (CDI), navigation
The JSF request-processing lifecycle, when to use JSF vs modern SPA + REST
Queues vs topics, ConnectionFactory, producers/consumers, JMS 3.0 simplified API
Message-driven beans, contrast Spring JMS / @JmsListener
Jakarta Security: HttpAuthenticationMechanism, IdentityStore, @RolesAllowed, contrast Spring Security
JSON Processing (JSON-P) and JSON Binding (JSON-B)
Concurrency Utilities (ManagedExecutorService, ManagedThreadFactory, @Asynchronous)
Jakarta WebSocket (@ServerEndpoint), Jakarta Mail overview
MicroProfile (Config, Health, Metrics, OpenAPI, Rest Client, Fault Tolerance, JWT)
Cloud-native Jakarta EE (Quarkus/Helidon/Open Liberty), Jakarta EE + MicroProfile vs Spring Boot
